steps.title: Use Desmos to fit the exponential table
steps.content: This is a table-to-exponential-model question, so enter the table rows in Desmos and regress. Use $x_1=[0,2]$ and $y_1=[1.25,1.80]$, then type $y_1 \sim a b^{x_1}$. Desmos gives $a=1.25$ and $b=1.20$, so the model is $h(x)=1.25(1.20)^x$.

steps.title: Check the model quickly
steps.content: The coefficient is correct because $h(0)=1.25(1.20)^0=1.25$. The base is correct because $h(2)=1.25(1.20)^2=1.25(1.44)=1.80$, matching the table.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: $1.20(0.25)^x$ has the wrong initial value and the base $0.25 < 1$ would shrink the heights instead of growing them. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: $1.20(1.25)^x$ has the wrong initial value $1.20$ at $x = 0$. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: $1.25(0.20)^x$ starts at the right value but the base $0.20$ would make heights collapse toward zero. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: $1.25(1.20)^x$ gives $h(0) = 1.25$ and $h(2) = 1.25(1.44) = 1.80$, matching the table. Matches.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
